question: Did the model's final path successfully connect the Start point (S) to the End point (E)?
Is the final answer exactly the optimal shortest path, consisting of 3 moves to the left (e.g., "left, left, left")?
Does the model's reasoning correctly identify that 'S' and 'E' are positioned on the same horizontal row or connected by a straight unobstructed corridor?
Does the model explicitly avoid taking unnecessary detours through the upper portions of the maze, recognizing the direct bottom route is optimal?
Is the drawn path in the generated image(s) clearly visible and distinguishable from the maze background?
Is the drawn path strictly contained within the open corridors without overlapping or crossing any black walls?
Does the drawn path appear as a straight horizontal line without unnecessary vertical deviations?
Does the path visually originate at 'S' and terminate at 'E' without gaps or discontinuities?
Does the generated image correctly anchor the path start at the 'S' cell (green area) and end at the 'E' cell (pink area)?
Do the text descriptions of movement (e.g., "move left") exactly match the direction of the line drawn in the generated image?
Is the number of moves stated in the text (3 moves) consistent with the visual length of the path (crossing 3 cells)?
Does the model avoid asserting it reached 'E' in text if the image path clearly stops short or ends elsewhere?
Are there any Text-Image Contradictions, such as the text stating "move up" while the image shows the path moving left?
